summary: Ukrainian forces reported numerous UAV attacks across multiple oblasts, while Russia launched a MiG-31K carrying an X-47M2 Kinzhal missile. The UN added Russian forces to a blacklist for verified sexual violence cases. Ukraine secured a pledge for 16 Gripen fighters from Sweden.
key_points: Multiple Shahed and other UAVs were shot down across northern, southern and eastern Ukraine, with 217 confirmed kills (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
A Russian MiG-31K took off from Savasleyka, capable of carrying the X-47M2 Kinzhal missile (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67254).
UN listed Russian armed forces for 310 verified sexual violence incidents in occupied Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81530).
Sweden announced delivery of 16 Gripen fighters to Ukraine, expected 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted a ballistic Iskander-M missile launched from Kursk region (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian UAV attacked a residential building in Galaţi, Romania, causing fire and casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).
Ukraine allocated 10.8 bn UAH for additional weapons procurement and defense industry development (Source: @DeepStateUA/23543).

sections.executive_summary: Multiple UAV attacks across Ukraine resulted in 217 enemy drones shot down, while Russia launched a MiG-31K with a Kinzhal missile. The UN added Russian forces to a blacklist for 310 verified sexual violence cases. Sweden pledged 16 Gripen fighters to Ukraine, slated for delivery in 2027.

points: Ukrainian air defenses downed 217 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other UAVs across the north, south and east, including 14 hits on 14 locations (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
The Ukrainian government allocated 10.8 bn UAH for new weapons, equipment repair and defense industry scaling, with 9 bn UAH for procurement (Source: @DeepStateUA/23543).

Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on multiple settlements: three Shahed drones near Vylkove, five over the sea toward Tatarbunari, and several over Odesa and Kharkiv regions (Source: @eRadarrua/81530).
Ukrainian officials noted a Russian UAV crash in Galaţi, Romania, causing a fire and injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).
Russia launched a MiG-31K from Savasleyka, capable of carrying the X-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67254).
A ballistic Iskander-M missile was fired from Kursk region toward Ukraine, intercepted by Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian tactical aviation conducted strikes on Zaporizhzhia region with guided aerial bombs (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Russian forces were added to the UN “black list” for verified sexual violence against POWs and civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81530).
UAVs reported over Sumy region: one near Lebedyn, another reactive from Sumy to Hadyach, and multiple Shahed drones near Shostka (Source: @eRadarrua/94085, @eRadarrua/94125).
A reactive UAV from Bryansk entered Chernihiv region, heading toward Horodnia (Source: @eRadarrua/94082).
UAVs observed over northern Chernihiv heading to Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/63183).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike destroying a school in Novhorod-Siverskyi district (Source: @UkraineNow/81526).
UAVs detected over northern Dnipropetrovsk heading to Cherkasy (Source: @kpszsu/63152).
UAVs over northern Poltava heading to Pyriatyn (Source: @kpszsu/63184).
UAVs over northern Chernihiv heading to Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/63183).
UAVs from southern Kharkiv heading to Slobozhanske (Source: @kpszsu/63161).
UAVs reported over Odesa region: three near Vylkove, five over the sea toward Tatarbunari, and additional attacks near Izmail (Source: @eRadarrua/81530, @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Eight Shahed drones spotted in the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi and Izmail districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Ten Shahed drones over the Black Sea toward Izmail district, plus five toward Orlivka/Reni (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67256).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted a ballistic missile and multiple UAVs in the south, including Shahed and Gerbera types (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Ukrainian air defense shot down 217 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and drone imitators (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
A Russian MiG-31K took off carrying a Kinzhal missile, indicating high‑speed strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67254).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a residential building in Galaţi, Romania, causing fire and casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses downed 217 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas types, across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
A Russian MiG-31K launched from Savasleyka, capable of carrying the X-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, took off (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67254).
The UN added Russian armed forces to a blacklist for 310 verified cases of sexual violence against POWs and civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81530).
Sweden pledged delivery of 16 Gripen fighters to Ukraine, expected in early 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
A ballistic Iskander-M missile was fired from Kursk region and intercepted by Ukrainian defenses (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
A Russian UAV crashed into a residential building in Galaţi, Romania, causing fire and injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).
Ukraine allocated 10.8 bn UAH for additional weapons procurement and defense industry development (Source: @DeepStateUA/23543).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial capabilities remain a significant threat, demonstrated by the MiG-31K launch and continued UAV mass attacks across Ukraine's southern and eastern fronts. The high volume of Shahed and other drones, coupled with ballistic missile use, stresses Ukrainian air defenses. Additionally, the UN's blacklist highlights systemic violations that may affect morale and international support.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering its air defense and procurement, evidenced by substantial budget allocations and the upcoming Gripen fighter deliveries from Sweden. NATO allies are monitoring cross-border incidents, such as the UAV strike in Romania, indicating heightened regional vigilance. International condemnation of Russian war crimes may increase diplomatic pressure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued tracking of UAV launch points in Russian regions (Kursk, Bryansk, Belgorod) is essential for pre‑emptive air defense positioning. Monitoring MiG-31K activity could reveal future hypersonic strike attempts. The UN's sexual violence findings suggest a coordinated intimidation strategy against POWs and civilians, requiring documentation and potential war‑crime investigations.
